label: Mennekens & Vanbeveren (2014)
parameter_family: CE efficiency / natal kick / mass transfer efficiency / remnant mass prescription / star formation history
parameter: α_CE, σ_kick, β, SFR model, fallback model, initial conditions

alpha_CE_note: α_CE is efficiency of transfer of orbital energy into CE escape energy (0 < α_CE < 1); values 0.1, 0.5, 1.0, and 5× multiplier tested across 23 models
beta_MT_note: case A is treated conservatively (β=1); for case Br: β=0.1, 0.5, or 1.0 (fraction accreted by gainer)
CE_prescription: Webbink (1984) alpha-lambda; Dewi & Tauris / Xu & Li lambda

lambda_CE: Dewi & Tauris (2000) for M<10 M⊙; Xu & Li (2010) for M>10 M⊙
lambda_CE_note: for stars below 10 M⊙ we implement results of Dewi & Tauris (2000), and for more massive stars those for Z=0.02 of Xu & Li (2010)
RMP: Woosley & Weaver (1995) model B; direct collapse for M_ZAMS≥40 M⊙
RMP_note: final CO-core to Fe-core via model B of Woosley & Weaver (1995); stars with initial mass ≥40 M⊙ collapse directly into BH
stellar_tracks_note: Brussels code (Vanbeveren et al.) detailed stellar evolution with mass-transfer and CE prescriptions

lambda_CE_note: (α,λ)=(1,0.1) for common envelope phase; Webbink (1984), de Kool (1992), Ivanova et al. (2013)

RMP_note: rapid core-collapse (default), delayed, StarTrack, compactness parameter O'Connor & Ott, two-parameter Ertl et al.

stellar_tracks_note: SEVN stellar evolution with PARSEC/PADOVA stellar evolution tracks

label: Belczynski et al. (2020)
parameter_family: angular momentum transport / remnant mass prescription / natal kick / stellar winds / star formation history
parameter: AM transport (Geneva/MESA/Fuller), PPSN prescription, σ_kick, stellar wind efficiency, metallicity evolution

alpha_CE: see CE_prescription; no explicit α_CE quoted

stellar_tracks_note: three AM transport models: Geneva (mild), MESA (efficient), Fuller et al. (super-efficient rotation)

parameter: AM transport (Geneva/MESA/Fuller), PPSN prescription, σ_kick, stellar wind efficiency, metallicity evolution
sigma_kick_note: high NS kicks σ=265 km s⁻¹ with fallback; small BH/NS kicks σ=70 km s⁻¹; intermediate σ=130 km s⁻¹
alpha_CE: see CE_prescription; no explicit α_CE quoted
alpha_CE_note: StarTrack CE uses Bondi-Hoyle accretion (5% or 10%) during CE instead of standard α efficiency; specific α not parameterized in this way
CE_prescription: Webbink (1984) alpha-lambda, 5% or 10% Bondi-Hoyle accretion in CE
CE_prescription_note: 5% Bondi-Hoyle accretion onto NS/BH in CE (models M30+); 10% Bondi-Hoyle in earlier models
RMP_note: MBH=(1-f_neu)Mstar; PPSN models use metallicity-dependent He-core mass relationships; rapid mass-gap SN engine
PISN_prescription: Woosley & Heger (2021) and variants; strong/moderate/weak PPSN mass loss
PISN_prescription_note: PPSN mass loss: strong, moderate, weak; metallicity-dependent He-core mass relationships (equations 7-9)
stellar_tracks_note: three AM transport models: Geneva (mild), MESA (efficient), Fuller et al. (super-efficient rotation)

RMP_note: stars with He core 64-135 M⊙ disrupted by pair instability; He 32-64 M⊙ → PPISN; direct collapse for M_CO>11 M⊙
stellar_tracks_note: SFRD: Madau & Fragos (2017) fitting formula; metallicity: De Cia et al. (2018) damped Ly-α, rescaled to Z(z=0)=(1.04±0.14)Z⊙

parameter_family: initial conditions / CE efficiency / natal kick / remnant mass prescription
parameter: initial binary distributions (Sana vs Moe), α_CE, CE optimistic/pessimistic, RMP, kick prescription
sigma_kick_note: iron core-collapse SNe kicks drawn from Maxwellian with σ=265 km s⁻¹; ECSN and ultra-stripped SNe: σ=20 km s⁻¹
sigma_stripped_SN_note: electron-capture SNe and ultra-stripped SNe given weaker kicks, σ=20 km s⁻¹
alpha_CE_note: CE efficiency parameterized as in Webbink (1984) and de Kool (1990); tested α=1.0 and α=5.0
CE_prescription_note: CE efficiency from Webbink (1984); optimistic (HG donors survive) and pessimistic (HG donors merge)
lambda_CE_note: variable prescription for the envelope binding energy factor λ (Claeys et al., 2014)
RMP: Fryer et al. (2012) rapid and delayed
RMP_note: both Rapid and Delayed SN prescriptions from Fryer et al. (2012) tested; also modified to cap neutronization at 10% iron-core mass
stellar_tracks_note: COSMIC population synthesis code using Hurley et al. (2002) BSE formulae

label: Ghodla et al. (2021)
parameter_family: remnant mass prescription / natal kick

alpha_CE_note: BPASS uses energy conservation CE; effective αλ inferred retroactively: effectively α_CE λ = 2-100
CE_prescription_note: BPASS implicit CE treatment; no explicit α_CE stated; prior BPASS work referenced
RMP: multiple: Standard binding energy, AlwaysNS, MCO,final, Fryer et al. (2012) rapid
RMP_note: 4 remnant mass schemes tested: Standard (binding energy), AlwaysNS (all 1.4 M⊙), MCO,final (CO core mass), FryerRapid (Fryer+2012)
stellar_tracks_note: updated rejuvenation during mass transfer onto secondary stars; improved GW merger time calculation via full Peters (1964) orbital evolution

label: Mapelli et al. (2021)
parameter_family: common envelope / mass transfer efficiency
sigma_kick_note: natal kicks from Giacobbo & Mapelli (2020) prescription (equation 7); relativistic kick v_kick=(v_m2+v_perp2+2v_m v_perp cosξ+v_par2)^0.5
alpha_CE_note: we parametrize common envelope evolution with the parameter α; here we consider α=1, 5, 10
beta_MT_note: accretion rate ṁ_a = {fMT|ṁ_d| if non-degenerate accretor; min(fMT|ṁ_d|,ṁ_Edd) otherwise}; fMT ∈ (0,1]
CE_prescription_note: large values of α mean the envelope is easily ejected; tested α=1, 5, 10
RMP: Fryer et al. (2012); PPISN αP correction
RMP_note: stars undergo PPISN (32≤mHe≤64); final mass ≈ 0.95 × total merging BH mass; αP≤1 pulsation parameter
PISN_prescription_note: stars with 32≤mHe≤64 M⊙ undergo pulsational pair instability; mBH=αP × mno,PPI
stellar_tracks_note: MOBSE code; natal kicks from Giacobbo & Mapelli (2020) prescription (equation 7, relativistic)

label: Mennekens & Vanbeveren (2014)
notes: 23 models varying: SFR, CE efficiency, MT efficiency, kick velocity, fallback, binary period distribution, LBV scenario, BH kicks
parameter_family: CE efficiency / natal kick / mass transfer efficiency / remnant mass prescription / star formation history
parameter: α_CE, σ_kick, β, SFR model, fallback model, initial conditions
sigma_kick_note: kick velocity distribution linked to observed pulsar velocity distribution, described with χ² distribution with average velocity 450 km/s; alternative: 265 km/s
alpha_CE_note: α_CE is efficiency of transfer of orbital energy into CE escape energy (0 < α_CE < 1); values 0.1, 0.5, 1.0, and 5× multiplier tested across 23 models
beta_MT_note: case A is treated conservatively (β=1); for case Br: β=0.1, 0.5, or 1.0 (fraction accreted by gainer)
CE_prescription: Webbink (1984) alpha-lambda; Dewi & Tauris / Xu & Li lambda
CE_prescription_note: energy balance CE described by Webbink (1984); λ from Dewi & Tauris (2000) (M<10 M⊙) and Xu & Li (2010) (M>10 M⊙)
lambda_CE: Dewi & Tauris (2000) for M<10 M⊙; Xu & Li (2010) for M>10 M⊙
lambda_CE_note: for stars below 10 M⊙ we implement results of Dewi & Tauris (2000), and for more massive stars those for Z=0.02 of Xu & Li (2010)
RMP: Woosley & Weaver (1995) model B; direct collapse for M_ZAMS≥40 M⊙
RMP_note: final CO-core to Fe-core via model B of Woosley & Weaver (1995); stars with initial mass ≥40 M⊙ collapse directly into BH
stellar_tracks_note: Brussels code (Vanbeveren et al.) detailed stellar evolution with mass-transfer and CE prescriptions

stellar_tracks_note: three AM transport models: Geneva (mild), MESA (efficient), Fuller et al. (super-efficient rotation)

stellar_tracks_note: COSMIC population synthesis code using Hurley et al. (2002) BSE formulae

label: Mennekens & Vanbeveren (2014)
parameter_family: CE efficiency / natal kick / mass transfer efficiency / remnant mass prescription / star formation history
parameter: α_CE, σ_kick, β, SFR model, fallback model, initial conditions
sigma_kick_note: kick velocity distribution linked to observed pulsar velocity distribution, described with χ² distribution with average velocity 450 km/s; alternative: 265 km/s
alpha_CE_note: α_CE is efficiency of transfer of orbital energy into CE escape energy (0 < α_CE < 1); values 0.1, 0.5, 1.0, and 5× multiplier tested across 23 models
beta_MT_note: case A is treated conservatively (β=1); for case Br: β=0.1, 0.5, or 1.0 (fraction accreted by gainer)
CE_prescription: Webbink (1984) alpha-lambda; Dewi & Tauris / Xu & Li lambda
CE_prescription_note: energy balance CE described by Webbink (1984); λ from Dewi & Tauris (2000) (M<10 M⊙) and Xu & Li (2010) (M>10 M⊙)
lambda_CE: Dewi & Tauris (2000) for M<10 M⊙; Xu & Li (2010) for M>10 M⊙
lambda_CE_note: for stars below 10 M⊙ we implement results of Dewi & Tauris (2000), and for more massive stars those for Z=0.02 of Xu & Li (2010)
RMP: Woosley & Weaver (1995) model B; direct collapse for M_ZAMS≥40 M⊙
RMP_note: final CO-core to Fe-core via model B of Woosley & Weaver (1995); stars with initial mass ≥40 M⊙ collapse directly into BH
stellar_tracks_note: Brussels code (Vanbeveren et al.) detailed stellar evolution with mass-transfer and CE prescriptions

RMP_note: stars with He core 64-135 M⊙ disrupted by pair instability; He 32-64 M⊙ → PPISN; direct collapse for M_CO>11 M⊙
stellar_tracks_note: SFRD: Madau & Fragos (2017) fitting formula; metallicity: De Cia et al. (2018) damped Ly-α, rescaled to Z(z=0)=(1.04±0.14)Z⊙

sigma_kick_note: iron core-collapse SNe kicks drawn from Maxwellian with σ=265 km s⁻¹; ECSN and ultra-stripped SNe: σ=20 km s⁻¹
sigma_stripped_SN_note: electron-capture SNe and ultra-stripped SNe given weaker kicks, σ=20 km s⁻¹

CE_prescription_note: CE efficiency from Webbink (1984); optimistic (HG donors survive) and pessimistic (HG donors merge)

RMP: Fryer et al. (2012) rapid and delayed

stellar_tracks_note: COSMIC population synthesis code using Hurley et al. (2002) BSE formulae

label: Chu et al. (2021)
parameter_family: common envelope / natal kick / mass transfer stability
parameter: α_CE or γ_AM, σ_kick, mass ejection fraction β
sigma_kick_note: we take standard value of σ_k=190 km s⁻¹ (Hansen & Phinney 1997); also σ_k=30 km s⁻¹ (low-kick) and bimodal models
alpha_CE_note: α-formalism: αCE=0.1, 1.0, 10.0 with λ=0.5; γ-formalism: J_i-J_f/J_i=γ(m1-m1c)/(m1+m2) with γ=1.1,1.3,1.5,1.7
CE_prescription: Webbink (1984) alpha-lambda (λ=0.5) or gamma angular momentum formalism
lambda_CE_note: λ=0.5 in α-formalism models
RMP_note: mNS=0.89+0.23mc,SN where mc,SN is CO-core mass at supernova explosion

parameter_family: star formation history / metallicity

beta_MT: during stable MT onto a stellar companion, accretion limited to $10\times$ the thermal rate of the accretor

stellar_tracks_note: fast algorithms following Hurley et al. (2000, 2002), based on Pols et al. (1998)

label: van Son et al. (2023)
notes: Stable MT channel: no CE events; varies β_acc (0-1), ζ_eff (3.5-6.5), q_crit, f_core, f_disk, RMP parameters (25 models)
parameter_family: mass transfer efficiency / mass transfer stability
parameter: β_acc (MT accretion efficiency), ζ_eff (donor response), q_crit, f_core, SN prescription fmix
sigma_kick: BH: fallback from Fryer et al. (2012) delayed model
sigma_kick_note: BH kicks according to the 'fallback' model from Fryer et al. (2012), where we adopt the proto-NS masses from the DELAYED model
beta_MT_note: fraction β_acc of the transferred mass will be accreted by the companion star; first MT: β=0.5 (fiducial); second MT: β=0 (Eddington limited)
gamma_AM: non-conservative MT mass is lost with the specific orbital AM of the accretor
CE_prescription: No CE — stable MT channel only
CE_prescription_note: every mass transfer episode throughout the binary evolution is dynamically stable, and no common envelope occurs; study focuses exclusively on the stable MT channel
RMP_note: primary: 'Delayed' model from Fryer et al. (2012) with linear approximation aSN=-0.9, bSN=13.9 M⊙, Mthresh=14.8 M⊙; also Fryer et al. (2022) with fmix variation tested
PISN_prescription: Farmer et al.~(2019); PPISN for He-core masses $>35\,\Msun$, PISN for $60$--$135\,\Msun$ \\
MT_stability_note: mass transfer is assumed stable as long as ζ_RL ≤ ζ_⋆; the stable MT channel requires all mass transfer phases to be dynamically stable
Eddington_limited_note: during the second mass transfer phase β=0 due to Eddington limited accretion onto the compact object
stellar_tracks: COMPAS (Hurley et al. 2002); stable MT channel only
stellar_tracks_note: COMPAS rapid population synthesis; focus on systems where all three mass transfer phases are dynamically stable
